3.happening: Initializing combined = [] — an empty list that will collect every value from both input lists.
3.why: We need a single container to hold all values before sorting them together.
3.invariant: combined is empty; no values have been gathered yet.

4.happening: Starting the first loop — iterating over list1 to copy its values into combined.
4.why: We must get all values from list1 into our shared container.
4.invariant: combined contains the values from list1 seen so far.

5.happening: Appending v = {v} from list1 to combined.
5.why: We gather every list1 value; the order does not matter yet because we will sort later.
5.invariant: combined grows by one; all values appended so far are from list1.

6.happening: Starting the second loop — iterating over list2 to copy its values into combined.
6.why: Now that list1 is fully copied, we add list2 values the same way.
6.invariant: combined already holds all list1 values; list2 values are being appended.

7.happening: Appending v = {v} from list2 to combined.
7.why: Adding list2 values one at a time; sorted order still does not matter here.
7.invariant: combined grows by one; it now holds some prefix of list2 plus all of list1.

8.happening: Sorting combined in-place — this is the O((n+m) log(n+m)) step.
8.why: The two original lists were individually sorted, but combined as a whole is not. Sorting now produces the merged sorted order.
8.invariant: After this line, combined is fully sorted in non-decreasing order.

9.happening: Assigning combined to result so the answer is stored in a named variable.
9.why: The visualizer and resultSpec track result specifically.
9.invariant: result is the sorted merged list — the final answer.

10.happening: Returning result — the merged sorted list.
10.why: The problem asks us to return the head of the merged list; here result is the complete merged sequence.
10.invariant: result contains all n+m values in non-decreasing order.

init.why: We need a fresh list to hold all values.

loop.why: Iterating over one of the input lists.

update.why: Either appending a value or sorting all collected values.
update.invariant: combined is growing or being sorted.

return.why: Both lists are fully merged and sorted.
return.invariant: result holds the complete sorted sequence.
